description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a tall, cylindrical black pole. The signal housing is rectangular and painted yellow, featuring three circular signal lights arranged vertically, with the top light illuminated red. The lights are encased in a slightly protruding frame, and the lenses are recessed for protection. The pole is ribbed for structural strength and ends in a wider, stable base. The back of the housing is flat and unadorned, while the sides show the depth of the signal box. This type of traffic light is commonly found at intersections to control vehicle flow.
description: A yellow, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights, the top one glowing red. The housing is rectangular and attached to a tall, ribbed black pole.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three signal lights, with the top light illuminated red and the yellow housing clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the yellow signal housing and the cylindrical black pole, with the lights visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, featureless back of the yellow housing and the full length of the supporting pole.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing the three signal lights protruding from the yellow housing and the ribbed pole.
